era_context: Early 6th Dynasty Egypt, Akkadian Empire in Mesopotamia, mature Harappan Indus Valley Civilization. The three major Old World civilizations are at or near their peak of urban development and territorial extent, though political fragmentation is beginning in Egypt.

events.title: 6th Dynasty Egypt: Continued administrative and military activity
events.description: The 6th Dynasty continues under Teti or Pepi I. Military expeditions are conducted in Nubia and the Levant. Trade networks are maintained with the Levant, the Red Sea region, and possibly the Indus Valley (via intermediaries). The pyramid of Pepi I at Saqqara is constructed during this period (if Pepi I is the reigning pharaoh). Provincial governors continue to gain power, setting the stage for the eventual fragmentation of the Old Kingdom.
key_figures: Teti or Pepi I (6th Dynasty pharaoh)
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for this year; the narrative is inferred from broader chronological context.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year.
historiographic_note: This year is not documented in any surviving source.
